query: What is the maximum capacity threshold for a  generator in MISO to be eligible for the fast-track interconnection process in MISO, according to MISO Tariff Attachment X Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP)? Cite the relevant source report and section for your answer
final_answer:
Based on my research, the maximum capacity threshold for a generator in MISO to be eligible for the fast-track interconnection process is **5 MW**.

**Source:** MISO Tariff Attachment X, Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP), **Section 14** (Fast Track Process).

**Supporting Evidence:**

1. **Direct statement from industry analysis** (Rao Konidena, June 12, 2026): "Under Section 14 of MISO's Attachment X, a Small Generating Facility no larger than 5 MW qualifies for the Fast Track Process — a 15-business-day initial review instead of the multi-year Definitive Planning Phase queue. The 5 MW line is a real regulatory breakpoint, and developers size to it." [Source: https://raokonidena.substack.com/p/why-don-t-we-see-more-energy-storage]

2. **Renewable Energy World article** (January 28, 2021): "Fast Track Process is available for renewable projects under 5 MW at MISO and SPP" and "MISO process only discusses 5 MW as the threshold for fast track process applicability." [Source: https://www.renewableenergyworld.com/solar/utility-scale/fast-track-process-is-available-for-renewable-projects-under-5-mw-at-miso-and-spp/]

3. **MISO Dashboard** (March 19, 2026): References "small generators 5 MW and under that are Fast Track eligible" in the context of GIA insurance requirements. [Source: https://www.misoenergy.org/engage/MISO-Dashboard/gia-small-generator-insurance-requirements-5-mw-and-under/]

4. **MISO Attachment X GIP (Effective January 31, 2025)**: Section 2.1(b) states that "Section 14 of the GIP applies to a request to interconnect a certified Small Generating Facility meeting the certification criteria set forth in Appendix 3." [Source: https://cdn.misoenergy.org/Attachment%20X%20-%20GIP%20(Effective%20January%2031%202025)330116.pdf]

The fast-track process under Section 14 provides an expedited path (initial review within 15 business days, with the entire process typically completed in under 90 days) for Small Generating Facilities ≤5 MW, as an alternative to the multi-year Definitive Planning Phase (DPP) queue process.

steps.reasoning:
I found a very clear reference in the search results. The Substack article by Rao Konidena (June 12, 2026) explicitly states:

"Under Section 14 of MISO's Attachment X, a Small Generating Facility no larger than 5 MW qualifies for the Fast Track Process — a 15-business-day initial review instead of the multi-year Definitive Planning Phase queue. The 5 MW line is a real regulatory breakpoint, and developers size to it."

This is a clear statement that the maximum capacity threshold for a generator in MISO to be eligible for the fast-track interconnection process is 5 MW, according to MISO Tariff Attachment X, Section 14.

Let me also check the Renewable Energy World article from 2021 which also confirms this: "Fast Track Process is available for renewable projects under 5 MW at MISO and SPP" and "MISO process only discusses 5 MW as the threshold for fast track process applicability."

And the MISO Dashboard page also mentions "small generators 5 MW and under that are Fast Track eligible."

So the answer is clearly 5 MW. The source is MISO Tariff Attachment X, Section 14 (Generator Interconnection Procedures- Fast Track Process).
